description: C-2 pin: a project switch WITHIN one session (ContextPulse window then StockTrader window, 5 min apart, same session). SPEC RESOLUTION (authoritative, resolves m4 vs §3.1): a re-derived active_project for the SAME open project pins valid_from=session_start (rule-1 fusion); a DIFFERENT project is a genuine mid-session transition and pins valid_from=observed_at, so rule 2's strict `>` fires — the old ContextPulse interval closes at the switch time (T0+5min) and a new StockTrader interval opens. Before the fix, m4 pinned EVERY active_project candidate to session_start, making candidate.valid_from == old.valid_from, which fell into rule 2b and built a zero-width [x,x) fact that violated the schema CHECK valid_to>valid_from and crashed ingest with IntegrityError (§1.2 'never zero-width'). Expected: two active_project eras, no crash. The StockTrader era is only [T0+5min, T0+5min+1) after flush (last-seen==valid_from => +1 zero-width rule). used_app app:code fuses across e1+e2 (0.95->0.9625). fact_count 4 = 1 session.occurred + 1 used_app + 2 active_project eras. Both project full-name tokens match window titles via >3-char containment (BD-1), so the rule-3 minor writes entity_aliases rows 'contextpulse' and 'stocktrader' (InsertAlias minor).
